_metadata.description: Quality control skill for AGENTS.md files. Validates against the contract in make_AGENTS.json.
_metadata.instructions: MD = the why and how (philosophy, pitfalls, examples). JSON = the rules, scoring, test cases. Update both files together.

skill_details.description: Validates AGENTS.md files against the contract in make_AGENTS.json. Sibling to make_agent_qc and make_gems/make_gem_qc.

agent_type.description: Rule-based validation system. 9 weighted rules across 6 quality dimensions. Score 0-100 with status mapping.

behavioral_discipline._description: make_AGENTS_qc operates as read_only — reads files, produces a report. No state changes.

_skipped_principles_rationale.P-002: No state changes to plan; QC reads + reports.
_skipped_principles_rationale.P-004: Root cause analysis is for bug-fixing; QC is for finding failures, not fixing them.
_skipped_principles_rationale.P-006: No documentation artifact produced beyond the report itself.

inputs.name: agents_md_path
inputs.format: Absolute path to the target AGENTS.md

inputs.name: repo_root
inputs.format: Absolute path to the repo root the AGENTS.md describes (optional but recommended — enables Genchi Genbutsu rules 7 and 9)

inputs.name: strict_mode
inputs.description: If true, fail on any critical or high-severity issue. Default: false (NEEDS_IMPROVEMENT for medium issues, FAIL only for <80 score).

outputs.name: validation_report
outputs.conditions: Always returned, even when AGENTS.md is malformed (with error status)

rules.name: Required Sections Present and In Order
rules.condition: AGENTS.md contains all 5 required sections from make_AGENTS.json → agents_md_required_sections.sections (title, project_purpose, structure, working_style, active_context) in that order. Headings match the contract exactly.
rules.action: Critical failure if any required section is missing. High failure if sections are present but out of order.

rules.name: Working Style Discipline Pointer
rules.condition: Working Style section references the behavioral discipline by file path — `knowledge/behavioral_discipline.md` (or an equivalent project-specific discipline source).
rules.action: Critical failure if no discipline reference exists. The Working Style section without a discipline pointer is a contract violation.

rules.name: Active Context Date Stamp Present
rules.condition: Active Context section starts with `_Last updated: YYYY-MM-DD_` (or close variant — italic markdown, ISO 8601 date).
rules.action: High failure if missing. Without a date stamp, freshness cannot be verified.

rules.name: No Tool-Specific Directive Language
rules.condition: AGENTS.md body does NOT contain phrases that direct one specific agentic tool. Example violations: 'When using Claude Code do X', 'In Cursor only', 'Aider should always'. Naming tools as a list of consumers is fine: 'readable by Claude Code, Cursor, Aider'.
rules.action: Medium failure per occurrence. Recommend rewording to tool-agnostic prose ('the LLM', 'your agentic tool').

rules.name: Optional Sections Meet Inclusion Criteria
rules.condition: For each optional section present in AGENTS.md (Domain Terms, External System Lessons, Migration Notes, Existing Tooling), the project must satisfy that section's `include_when` criterion in make_AGENTS.json → agents_md_optional_sections. Empty rows or placeholder content (e.g., `[TERM] | [Definition]`) count as failure to meet criterion.
rules.action: Medium failure per orphaned optional section. Recommend removal.

rules.name: Discipline Pointer Quality (Not a Duplicate)
rules.condition: Working Style section contains the discipline pointer plus AT MOST ~3 sentences of original prose about the discipline. More than that is paraphrasing the source — which rots when the source updates.
rules.action: High failure if Working Style contains >3 sentences of original prose paraphrasing the principles or restating the override rules. Recommend replacing the paraphrase with a pure pointer.

rules.name: Structure Section Matches Repo State (Genchi Genbutsu)
rules.condition: When repo_root is provided: the file paths and folder structure listed in AGENTS.md → Structure section match the actual tracked content (per `git ls-files`). Tracked files mentioned in AGENTS.md must exist; major tracked folders must appear in the tree. **In-flight content exception**: files that exist on disk but are not yet tracked are NOT drift if AGENTS.md → Active Context (or Migration Notes) explicitly flags them as in-flight / about-to-commit. The reader can correlate the Structure listing with the in-flight context.
rules.action: High failure for each non-trivial drift (gitignored files presented as tracked, listed files that don't exist, tracked content that's missing from the tree). Low/info severity for uncommitted-but-imminent files when in-flight context covers them. Skip this rule with a note when repo_root is not provided.

rules.name: Active Context Date Stamp Within Freshness Threshold
rules.condition: The date stamp in Active Context is within 90 days of today. Stamps older than 90 days flag the AGENTS.md as stale; the content claims may not reflect current state.
rules.action: Medium failure for stamps 90-180 days old. High failure for stamps >180 days old.

rules.name: Specificity — No Template Placeholders
rules.condition: AGENTS.md body does NOT contain placeholder patterns: `[Project Name]`, `[Description]`, `<your_*>`, `{{...}}` substitution markers, `TODO:`, `FIXME:`. Headings can contain placeholder-style text only if the section is intentionally kept as scaffolding.
rules.action: Medium failure per placeholder. High failure if placeholders appear in required sections (1-5).

rule_based.default_action: If all rules pass, score 100. If any rule with severity=critical fails, score caps at 70 regardless of other rules.

primary_data._description: Pattern data used by the rules.
primary_data._tool_specific_pattern_note: These patterns catch tool-NAME-as-actor language. Patterns like 'readable by Claude Code, Cursor, Aider, Antigravity' (tools as a list of consumers) are not violations and are not in this list.

validation_rules_detailed._description: The 6 quality dimensions and how rules map to them.

checks: All 5 required sections present and in order
Structure section accurately reflects the repo (when repo accessible)
Concrete project content, not template scaffolding
Working Style references the discipline by path
Pointer-quality, not duplicate-quality (no inline paraphrasing of principles)
Active Context has a date stamp
No tool-specific directive language in body
Tool names appear only as a list of consumers, not as actors
Each present optional section meets its include_when criterion
No empty or placeholder rows in optional sections

severity_levels._canonical_scoring_model: ONE scoring path. Each dimension starts at 100. For each rule that fails, deduct from its dimension based on severity (per-occurrence). Dimension score is floored at 0. Overall score = weighted average of dimension scores using the dimension weights below (which sum to 1.0). Status is determined by overall score AND critical-issue presence (a single critical → FAIL regardless of score). Per-issue deduction values are listed below; do not apply additional deductions outside this model.

deductions_per_failed_rule._note: Deductions apply within the failed rule's mapped dimension only. A critical failure zeroes its dimension.

severity_levels.strict_mode_override: If strict_mode=true, status downgrades to FAIL on any critical OR high-severity issue regardless of score.

output_format._description: Standard report format used by every QC run. Per P-008 — same shape every time.

summary.rules_skipped: array of rule_ids skipped (e.g. [7] if no repo_root)

issue_object_shape.location: string (line number or section name in AGENTS.md)

report_template.recommendations: array of strings — overall guidance prioritized by impact
report_template.worth_noting: string or null — per P-009, surface non-obvious authorship lessons here

pre_run_checklist: agents_md_path exists and is a readable file
If repo_root provided, the directory exists and `git ls-files` runs
post_run_checklist: Issues are sorted by severity (critical first)
Each issue references a rule_id and includes a recommendation
Score is calculated correctly per the deduction rules
success_criteria: AGENTS.md with deliberate violations (placeholder rows, no date stamp, structure drift) scores <70
Same input → same output (deterministic per P-008)

test_cases.name: Validate this repo's AGENTS.md (the iter-3 ship)
Reject AGENTS.md with placeholder rows in Domain Terms

input.agents_md_path: /tmp/no_discipline_agents.md (Working Style section has no reference to knowledge/behavioral_discipline.md)

test_cases.name: Detect Structure section drift via Genchi Genbutsu

input.agents_md_path: /path/to/stale-agents.md (lists folder X that no longer exists)

test_cases.name: Skip rule 7 cleanly when repo_root not provided

tolerance.numeric: Score ranges allow ±2 points for rounding
tolerance.structural: Issue array length comparisons allow exact match

commands: Conversational invocation through any agentic dev tool — see make_AGENTS_qc.md → How to Use This Skill

when_to_use: After generating a new AGENTS.md via make_AGENTS
Before merging a PR that modifies AGENTS.md
When an LLM gives clearly wrong project-state answers (suspected drift)
best_practices: Run with repo_root set whenever possible — Rule 7 catches the highest-impact drift
Use non-strict mode during development; strict mode for release gates
Fix critical issues first (Rules 1, 2). Most other issues become trivial once those are resolved.
Keep a known-good AGENTS.md (the one in this repo, scoring ≥95) as a regression baseline.

changes: 9 rules across 6 quality dimensions: completeness, specificity, discipline_integration, currency, tool_agnosticism, optional_section_justification.

Rule 7 (Structure Genchi Genbutsu) catches drift between AGENTS.md claims and actual repo state when repo_root is provided. Includes an in-flight-content exception for files documented as about-to-commit.
Rule 6 catches the over-paraphrasing-Working-Style failure mode observed during AGENTS.md iteration 1.
Skill itself operates as interaction_pattern: read_only with applicable_principles P-001, P-003, P-007, P-008, P-009, P-010 (skip P-002, P-004, P-005, P-006).

Single canonical scoring model: dimensions start at 100, deductions per failed rule by severity (-100 critical / -25 high / -10 medium / -3 low) within the rule's dimension; overall score = weighted dimension average; critical issue → FAIL regardless of score.
Output format is fixed (P-008 Mistake-Proof Outputs): every report has the same structure.
